section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports that she is atopic retinoid will cause some sensitivity, so she will need to wear sunscreen when she is outside.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is counseling about expected side effects and the need to wear sunscreen, which is part of management/plan rather than a patient-reported symptom or history.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports that she is experiencing sensitivity.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Framed as a current experience, this could be subjective, but in context it appears to restate anticipated treatment side effects rather than a clearly reported symptom; it would better fit as part of the clinician's assessment of treatment tolerability.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports that she is experiencing dryness.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Similar to the sensitivity statement, this reads more like a restatement of expected medication side effects than a clearly documented patient complaint; it would be clearer in the assessment of treatment effects.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports that she is experiencing irritation.
section_miscategorizations.reason: As with the prior two, this appears to echo anticipated retinoid side effects rather than a distinct patient-reported symptom; it would be better organized under assessment of medication tolerance.
